title: elaeocarpus teysmannii subsp. teysmannii
intro: Elaeocarpus teysmannii subsp. teysmannii, also known as the Mount Tahan tree, is a species of flowering plant in the Elaeocarpaceae family, native to Borneo and Sumatra in Indonesia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus teysmannii subsp. teysmannii include its potential to reduce inflammation, prevent bacterial infections, and exhibit antioxidant activity, which can contribute to the protection of cells from oxidative damage. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are consumed to treat various health conditions.
